//: ALL-OWN hardening: this chip placed and routed out of the pinned
stdcells release (lib.lock), with zero foundry cells of any kind.
Ported from stdcells harden/config.json at tag lib-v1.0 — the
settings below are that repo's hard-won ones, not fresh guesses.
Synthesis already happened in flow/make_hardening.py; CI does P&R
only, so the netlist that gets audited is the netlist that ships.
TT 1x1 tile. The logic alone is 83.4% of a 1x1 core before CTS and
hold fixing, so this is an experiment: if placement or routing dies,
switch to the 1x2 line below (161.00 x 225.76) and note it in PLAN.
Core margins: librelane's defaults eat ~25% of a 1x1 (site HEIGHTS
of 10.9um each). 1/1/2/2 was the lever that won the stdcells 1x1.
A fast library makes hold OVERFIX expensive: default margins x a
171ps BUF_X2 produced 322 repair buffers = 27% of logic area and an
infeasible placement. 0.005 is the measured working value.
THE zero-foundry lever: without this, the flow quietly inserts hd
tie cells, hold buffers, CTS buffers and fill, and the claim dies.
Signoff is the official KLayout deck, run as its own CI step (see
harden.yml). magic's CIF read of GDS-only custom cells emits tens of
thousands of phantom errors, and netgen LVS rides on that same
extraction — cell-level LVS was done in stdcells against the PDK deck.
